message: docs: redesign site for CLI and extension

-

CLI

-

Link → clean text → summary.

-

- A pragmatic pipeline for the web: extract the good stuff, sanitize it, - then summarize with your model of choice. Podcast episode pages included. Plus a Chrome - side panel extension when you want it in-browser. -

- -
- Website extraction - YouTube transcripts - Podcast compatibility - JSON + metrics - Chrome extension +
+
+

CLI + Chrome Extension

+

Summaries that live where you work.

+

+ Summarize turns links, files, and media into sharp summaries with a real extraction pipeline. + Use the CLI for automation or the Chrome Side Panel for one-click + summaries of the current tab. +

+
+ Docs + GitHub +
+
+
Quickstart
+
+ npm i -g @steipete/summarize + summarize "https://example.com/article"
- -
- - - Docs - - - - Repo - +

+
-
-

Quickstart

+
+ + +
+
+
-
-
- - - -
- 
npm i -g @steipete/summarize
-summarize "https://example.com/article"
-
+
+
+
+ CLI +

Fast summaries, scripted or interactive.

+
+

+ Built for automation: extract clean text, summarize with your model, and output JSON or Markdown. + Works with URLs, PDFs, images, audio/video, YouTube, and podcasts. +

+
    +
  • Extract + summarize with Firecrawl fallback.
    • +
  • Media pipeline with transcript-first flow and Whisper fallback.
    • +
  • Scriptable output via --json, --extract, --metrics.
    • +
+
+ 
summarize "https://example.com" --length long
+summarize "https://youtu.be/..." --youtube auto
+summarize "/path/report.pdf" --model google/gemini-3-flash-preview
+
+
+ CLI docs + Config +
+
-
- Install, then summarize a URL. -
- - -
-
- +
+
+ Chrome Extension +

Summaries in the Side Panel, one click away.

- +

+ A real Chrome Side Panel with a tiny local daemon. It streams Markdown summaries for the active tab, + with auto-summary on navigation. +

+
    +
  1. Install the CLI + daemon.
    2. +
  2. Load the unpacked extension.
    3. +
  3. Open Side Panel and connect with the token.
    4. +
+
+ Runs locally on your machine. The daemon is localhost-only and token-protected. +
+
+ Extension docs + Latest release +
+
-
-
-

Extraction that’s not naive

-

HTML → normalized text, with fallbacks when a site fights back.

-
Cheerio + sanitization + optional Firecrawl
+
+
+ Extraction +

HTML -> clean text -> summary

+

Readability, markitdown, and Firecrawl fallback when sites fight back.

-
-

Podcast-friendly

-

Apple/Spotify/RSS episode pages, plus embedded YouTube podcasts.

-
Prefers published transcripts; Whisper fallback
+
+ Media +

Podcast + YouTube aware

+

Prefers published transcripts, then Whisper when needed.

-
-

YouTube transcripts, without drama

-

Pull captions via multiple paths so “missing transcript” becomes rare.

-
Website vs YouTube modes
+
+ Models +

Provider-agnostic

+

OpenAI, Anthropic, Google, xAI, Z.AI, OpenRouter. Auto or pinned.

-
-

Built for pipelines

-

--extract, --json, and --metrics make it scriptable.

-
Compose it with your own tools
+
+ Outputs +

Readable + scriptable

+

Streaming TTY output, ANSI Markdown, JSON diagnostics, and metrics.

-
-

Provider-agnostic LLMs

-

OpenAI, xAI, Gemini — use what you’ve got, pin what you need.

-
Control via --model + env vars

+

How it works

+
+
+ 01 +
+

Fetch + extract

+

Pull the source, clean HTML, normalize, or convert to Markdown.

+
+
+
+ 02 +
+

Transcript when needed

+

Use published transcripts, then Whisper fallback for media.

+
+
+
+ 03 +
+

Summarize + format

+

LLM output, streaming in the CLI or Side Panel, with metrics.

Summarize the active tab without leaving your browser.

- Companion extension for the CLI. Download from the - latest GitHub release. +
